---
title: "American Community Survey (ACS) 5-year Estimates"
categories:
  - Census
data_description: '"The [American Community Survey](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/about.html) (ACS) is an ongoing survey that provides vital information on a yearly basis about our nation and its people. Information from the survey generates data that help determine how more than \$675 billion in federal and state funds are distributed each year.” (US Census Bureau)'
data_time_frame: "5 Years; 2009 to 2024"
data_spatial_scale: "Nation, State (including DC and Puerto Rico), Metropolitan Area, Congressional District, County, Place, Census Tract and Block Group"
data_format: "Feature Layers"
data_source: "US Census Bureau"
data_orig_repo: "[All Years](https://data.census.gov/)"
data_living_atlas: "[Current](https://www.arcgis.com/home/group.html?id=16b3a5ac36834ed6b3f16261d9a988ea#overview)"
data_details: |
  The Living Atlas hosts the current ACS 5-year estimates in a series of several features grouped by topic. Each topic is hosted as both [boundaries and centroids](https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/41743d0d5364488fb51d795d65b40b37). Topics may contain single or multiple ACS tables Layers can be [accessed and used](https://www.arcgis.com/apps/Cascade/index.html?appid=c6a35a434a8f4913b9c35022290efdd0) within your GIS workflows, for example, [make a map about your community](https://www.esri.com/arcgis-blog/products/arcgis-living-atlas/mapping/make-an-acs-map-fast/).

  If you are looking for historical data, the census.gov website can be difficult to go through. [Census Reporter](https://censusreporter.org/) can make it easier to find and download tables at various spatial scales.
format:
  html:
    template: ../../assets/data_catalog/data_template.html
---
